text:
The wind gently crinkled the autumn leaves. Old Mr. Willowby sat alone in his old oak study. He had lived here since his father's death, a man who once ran a successful brewery. Now old Mr. Willowby grew fatter each year. He was an eccentric man, loved by neighbors for his mysterious tales. He'd tell them about the ghosts in the attic, the secret garden outside his window, and his favorite ghost—The Shadow.

One afternoon, looking out his window toward Owl Creek Park, Mr. Willowby received a letter. It was from an old friend, Alice. They hadn't seen each other since Alice had settled in London. She wrote that her mother was terribly ill. Her father wanted to visit, hoping to comfort her.

His heartstrings tugged. Mr. Willowby was going on a long trip—a journey deep into the heart of England. He decided he must see Alice.

Upon his return, he visited Alice first. She had moved
